items.q: How do you say 'I study maths' in French?
items.why: Students drop the article like English. School subjects in French take the DEFINITE article: 'j'aime le français', 'j'étudie les sciences', 'je déteste l'histoire'. This is the rule for general nouns.

items.q: How do you say 'I am going to visit Paris' (near future) in French?
items.why: Students add 'à' between aller and the infinitive. Futur proche structure is ALLER (conjugated) + INFINITIVE, with no preposition: 'je vais manger', 'je vais aller', 'je vais partir'.

Students default to à. The rule: EN for feminine countries (most ending -e: France, Italie, Espagne); AU for masculine (au Portugal, au Canada); AUX for plural (aux États-Unis). À is for cities (à Paris).

items.q: Which sentence means 'I prefer geography because it's interesting'?
items.correct: Je préfère la géographie parce que c'est intéressant
items.misconception: J'aime la géographie parce que c'est ennuyeux
items.random_wrong: Je déteste la géographie parce que c'est intéressant
items.why: It's Je préfère la géographie parce que c'est intéressant. “J'aime la géographie parce que c'est ennuyeux” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How do you say 'on Mondays I have maths at 9'?
items.correct: Le lundi, j'ai maths à neuf heures
items.random_wrong: Sur lundi, j'ai des maths à neuf heures
items.why: It's Le lundi, j'ai maths à neuf heures. “Lundi j'ai maths à neuf” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: If a French school day starts at 8h30 and ends at 16h00 with 1h for lunch, how many hours of lessons?
items.why: The real figure is 6.5. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
